content.text: Secrets Scanning CI Gates for AI Repos

AI application repositories are high-value targets for credential theft because they contain API keys that directly translate to compute cost and data access. An Anthropic API key exposed in a public repo can be scraped within minutes by automated bots that watch GitHub pushes. Secrets scanning gates prevent keys from ever reaching version control, and incident response automation minimizes the blast radius when they do.

attrs.alt: Secrets scanning defense-in-depth: four layers from pre-commit to runtime

content.text: What Counts as a Secret in AI Repos
Secret Type
Example Pattern
Risk if Leaked
Anthropic API key
sk-ant-api03-...
Unbounded API cost, model access, usage data exposure
OpenAI / other LLM keys
sk-... or similar
Same — cost and access
Database credentials
DATABASE_URL with password
Direct data access
Cloud provider keys
AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID / AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
Full account compromise
OAuth tokens
ghp_... or xoxb-...
Repository or Slack workspace access
Private SSH/TLS keys
-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
Infrastructure access
Webhook secrets
WEBHOOK_SECRET=...
Request forgery

Anthropic API keys are especially sensitive because they are metered by token usage. A leaked key can result in thousands of dollars in charges before the abuse is detected. GitHub's native secret scanning detects Anthropic key patterns and notifies Anthropic, who may auto-revoke the key — but the goal is to never expose the key at all.
Layer 1: Pre-commit Hook with detect-secrets

pip install detect-secrets pre-commit

# Initialize a baseline (allowlist of known safe false positives)
detect-secrets scan > .secrets.baseline

# Review and audit the baseline
detect-secrets audit .secrets.baseline

# .pre-commit-config.yaml
repos:
  - repo: https://github.com/Yelp/detect-secrets
    rev: v1.4.0
    hooks:
      - id: detect-secrets
        args: ['--baseline', '.secrets.baseline']
        # Exclude test fixtures that intentionally contain fake secrets
        exclude: |
          (?x)^(
            tests/fixtures/.*|
            docs/examples/.*
          )$

  - repo: https://github.com/gitleaks/gitleaks
    rev: v8.18.0
    hooks:
      - id: gitleaks

# Install hooks for every developer
pre-commit install
pre-commit install --hook-type commit-msg

# Test that hooks catch a real secret pattern
echo 'ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-api03-fake-key-for-testing' > /tmp/test_secret.py
pre-commit run detect-secrets --files /tmp/test_secret.py
# Expected: detect-secrets FAILED
Layer 2: CI Gate on Every PR

# .github/workflows/secrets-scan.yml
name: Secrets Scan

on:
  pull_request: {}
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  detect-secrets:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0  # full history for diff scanning

      - name: Install detect-secrets
        run: pip install detect-secrets

      - name: Scan changed files only (fast)
        run: |
          # On PR: scan only the files changed in this PR
          if [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "pull_request" ]; then
            git diff --name-only origin/${{ github.base_ref }}...HEAD > /tmp/changed_files.txt
            detect-secrets scan --list-all-plugins \
              --baseline .secrets.baseline \
              $(cat /tmp/changed_files.txt | tr '\n' ' ') \
              > /tmp/scan_result.json
          else
            detect-secrets scan --baseline .secrets.baseline \
              > /tmp/scan_result.json
          fi

      - name: Check for new secrets
        run: |
          # detect-secrets exits non-zero if new secrets found vs baseline
          detect-secrets audit /tmp/scan_result.json --only-allowlisted
          python - <<'EOF'
          import json, sys
          with open("/tmp/scan_result.json") as f:
              data = json.load(f)
          results = data.get("results", {})
          new_secrets = {k: v for k, v in results.items() if v}
          if new_secrets:
              for path, findings in new_secrets.items():
                  for f in findings:
                      print(f"SECRET FOUND: {f['type']} in {path}:{f['line_number']}")
              sys.exit(1)
          print("No secrets detected.")
          EOF

  trufflehog:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - name: TruffleHog scan
        uses: trufflesecurity/trufflehog@main
        with:
          path: ./
          base: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}
          head: HEAD
          extra_args: --only-verified  # only report verified active secrets
Layer 3: Weekly Full History Scan
PR-level scanning only catches new secrets. A weekly scan of the entire git history finds secrets committed before the gate was in place, or pushed to branches that bypass normal PR review.

# .github/workflows/history-scan.yml
name: Full History Secret Scan

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 3 * * 0'   # Sunday 03:00 UTC
  workflow_dispatch: {}

jobs:
  history-scan:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0  # all history

      - name: TruffleHog full history
        run: |
          docker run --rm \
            -v "$PWD:/repo" \
            trufflesecurity/trufflehog:latest \
            git file:///repo \
            --only-verified \
            --json \
            > /tmp/trufflehog_history.json || true

      - name: Parse and alert
        run: |
          python - <<'EOF'
          import json, sys
          findings = []
          with open("/tmp/trufflehog_history.json") as f:
              for line in f:
                  line = line.strip()
                  if line:
                      try:
                          findings.append(json.loads(line))
                      except json.JSONDecodeError:
                          pass
          if findings:
              print(f"ALERT: {len(findings)} verified secret(s) in git history!")
              for f in findings:
                  print(f"  [{f.get('DetectorName')}] {f.get('SourceMetadata', {}).get('Data', {}).get('Git', {}).get('file','?')}")
              sys.exit(1)
          print("No verified secrets found in git history.")
          EOF

      - name: Upload report
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: history-scan-report
          path: /tmp/trufflehog_history.json
Custom gitleaks Config for AI Repos
Extend gitleaks with custom rules to catch AI-specific secret patterns that generic rules miss.

# .gitleaks.toml
[extend]
useDefault = true  # include all default gitleaks rules

[[rules]]
id = "anthropic-api-key"
description = "Anthropic API Key"
regex = '''sk-ant-api[0-9]{2}-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{95}'''
tags = ["anthropic", "api-key", "ai"]
severity = "CRITICAL"

[[rules]]
id = "openai-api-key"
description = "OpenAI API Key"
regex = '''sk-[A-Za-z0-9]{48}'''
tags = ["openai", "api-key", "ai"]
severity = "CRITICAL"

[[rules]]
id = "anthropic-key-in-env"
description = "Anthropic key assigned in .env or config"
regex = '''ANTHROPIC_API_KEY\s*=\s*["']?sk-[A-Za-z0-9_-]+["']?'''
tags = ["anthropic", "env"]
severity = "CRITICAL"

[allowlist]
description = "Test fixtures and documentation examples"
regexes = [
  '''sk-ant-api03-EXAMPLE''',
  '''sk-ant-PLACEHOLDER''',
  '''YOUR_API_KEY_HERE''',
]
paths = [
  '''tests/fixtures/''',
  '''docs/''',
  '''README''',
]
Incident Response: Key Leaked in a Commit
When a secret is found in a commit — whether by a scanner or reported externally — the response is always the same sequence: revoke first, then investigate.
Immediately revoke the leaked key at console.anthropic.com — do not wait for investigation.
Generate and deploy a replacement key to all environments that used the old one.
Determine the blast radius: when was the key first committed, was the repo public at any point, do access logs show usage from unexpected IPs or regions?
If the repo is public: treat the key as fully compromised regardless of when the commit was made — automated scanners index GitHub in real time.
Remove the secret from git history using git-filter-repo (not git filter-branch).
Force-push the rewritten history. All collaborators must re-clone.
Add the specific pattern to the .secrets.baseline allowlist with a note explaining it was a revoked key.
File a post-mortem: how did the key end up committed, which layer failed, what process change prevents recurrence?

# Remove a secret from all git history (run on a fresh clone)
pip install git-filter-repo

# Create a file with the secret string to remove
echo 'sk-ant-api03-YOUR_LEAKED_KEY_HERE' > /tmp/secrets_to_remove.txt

git filter-repo \
  --replace-text /tmp/secrets_to_remove.txt \
  --force

# This rewrites every commit that contained the string.
# After rewriting, force-push all branches:
git push origin --force-with-lease --all
git push origin --force-with-lease --tags

# All collaborators must run:
# git clone <repo-url> (fresh clone — existing clones have the old history)
Preventing Accidental Secrets: Safe Patterns
Anti-pattern
Safe alternative
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY = "sk-ant-..."  # hardcoded
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY = os.environ["ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"]
client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key='sk-...')
client = anthropic.Anthropic()  # reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from env automatically
.env file committed to git
.env listed in .gitignore; .env.example with placeholder values committed
API key in pytest fixture file
Fixture reads from environment: api_key = os.environ.get('ANTHROPIC_API_KEY', 'test-key')
Key in CI workflow env block
Key in GitHub Secrets: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
Key printed in debug log
Log only response.usage, response.stop_reason — never the request headers
Secrets in Test Files and Fixtures

Test suites are a common location for accidentally committed secrets. Test fixtures may use real API keys copied from a developer's environment, or integration tests may expect a key to be present and developers hardcode it to make the test pass locally.

# tests/conftest.py — safe pattern for API key in tests
import os
import pytest

@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def anthropic_api_key() -> str:
    """
    Read API key from environment only — never hardcode.
    Tests that require a real key are marked with @pytest.mark.integration
    and skipped in CI unless ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set.
    """
    key = os.environ.get("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY")
    if key is None:
        pytest.skip("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set — skipping integration test")
    return key

@pytest.fixture
def mock_api_key() -> str:
    """
    For unit tests that don't call the real API.
    A recognizable fake key that scanners can be trained to ignore.
    Never use a real key format.
    """
    return "sk-ant-PLACEHOLDER-FOR-TESTING-ONLY"

Add fake key patterns used in tests to the .secrets.baseline allowlist and the gitleaks allowlist. That way scanners don't cry wolf on known-safe test fixtures, while still catching real keys with the same format.

# Update .secrets.baseline after adding a new allowed test pattern
detect-secrets scan --baseline .secrets.baseline tests/
# Then audit interactively to mark new findings as allowed:
detect-secrets audit .secrets.baseline
# Commit the updated baseline
git add .secrets.baseline
git commit -m "security: allowlist test fixture key placeholder"
GitHub Native Secret Scanning

Enable GitHub's built-in secret scanning on every repository. It scans all commits in real time and notifies you — and sometimes the credential provider directly — when a known secret format is detected.

# .github/secret_scanning.yml
# Paths to exclude from GitHub native secret scanning
# (test fixtures with documented fake values)
paths-ignore:
  - 'tests/fixtures/**'
  - 'docs/examples/**'
Go to Settings → Security → Secret scanning → Enable for the repository.
Enable "Push protection" to block pushes that contain detected secrets before they are written to the remote.
Configure alert notifications to go to the security team email alias, not just the committer.
Review and close false-positive alerts promptly — a high false-positive rate causes teams to ignore real alerts.
Anthropic is a registered GitHub secret scanning partner. GitHub will notify Anthropic when an Anthropic key pattern is detected, which may trigger automatic revocation.
Summary

Secrets scanning for AI repos requires four defense layers: pre-commit hooks that block commits containing key patterns, CI gates that scan every PR diff with detect-secrets and TruffleHog, weekly full-history scans that catch secrets in older commits, and runtime practices that keep keys in environment variables and secret managers rather than code. Extend default scanner rules with custom patterns for Anthropic key formats. Manage test fixture false positives through baselines and allowlists rather than raising thresholds. When a key does leak, revoke it before investigating. Use git-filter-repo to rewrite history, then post-mortem the process failure.
